findings.summary: Unresolved: the launcher and Slurm job can execute inherited, unreviewed environment hooks before the reviewed authorization and checksum checks run.
evidence: scripts/hpc/submit_research_run.sh:4-7 accepts HPC_SOCIALITY_ROOT to select the launcher checkout and invokes an unbound PATH-resolved python3, despite the v1.5 contract declaring managed-base override forbidden.

scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:1117-1146 renders a /usr/bin/env bash job without #SBATCH --export=NONE and only sets thread/PYTHONPATH variables after shell startup; scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:1196 submits with sbatch's inherited-environment default.

Consequently BASH_ENV, ENV, LD_PRELOAD, PATH-selected bash/tools, PYTHONHOME, and related hooks can act before the script's git/hash/formal-G1 checks or alter them. The checksum-bound python3.9 at lines 1109-1112 protects the later executable bytes, not the shell/launcher path that decides whether and what to submit.

No reviewed test asserts a clean submission environment, --export=NONE, absolute shell/tool paths, rejection of HPC_SOCIALITY_ROOT, or invocation of the launcher with the bound interpreter. This must be fixed and adversarially tested before formal G1 can pass.

findings.summary: Unresolved: the reviewed-byte manifest is a required-file subset, not a closed Python execution/import set.
evidence: scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:746-756 proves that every reviewed file is present with the reviewed digest, but it permits arbitrary additional files in the published commit and final checksum bundle.

The rendered job exports PYTHONPATH=<detached code>:<detached code>/src at lines 1138-1139 and launches Python without -I/-S at lines 1037-1061. An additional sitecustomize.py, usercustomize.py, or stdlib-name shadow module in an otherwise published clean commit can therefore execute even though it is absent from the formal review manifest.

The environment manifest schema binds only the python executable, version, and a declaration that standard-library-only dependencies are used; it does not bind the imported standard-library module bytes or prove an isolated import closure.

The execution path should use isolated/no-site startup with an explicitly constructed reviewed module path (or an equivalent closed import manifest/tree digest), and tests should demonstrate rejection or non-loading of unreviewed sitecustomize and shadow-module fixtures.

findings.summary: Submission-preparation failures are fail-closed but do not have a deterministic rollback or structured FAILED receipt.
evidence: scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:1158-1196 creates the run directories and detached worktree before sbatch, without a compensating cleanup/failure-record block. A worktree-add, manifest-write, render, chmod, or sbatch error can leave an orphaned run root that prevents reuse of the immutable run_id.

The exporter itself has appropriate staging cleanup and removes the published bundle if run-receipt creation fails at scripts/rq014/export_score_stripped_bundle.py:417-421,598-602,639-671, so the data product remains fail-closed. The remaining issue is operational auditability and retry determinism, not rating leakage.

findings.summary: Authorization precedence, formal-review staleness checks, source bindings, and the prior-export receipt chain are otherwise fail-closed in the frozen bytes.
evidence: configs/research_authorization.json centrally allowlists only rq014_g2_declassification_export; reports/plans/RQ014_execution_contract_v1p5.json keeps preflight, pilot, feature, rating, and replay operations denied.
scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:362-451 binds formal G1 to two exact reviewer artifacts and one exact review manifest; lines 744-756 require the final bundle to preserve every reviewed digest.

scripts/hpc/prepare_research_run.py:593-677 requires the exact audited shard_0..7/readiness/counterpart paths, sizes, and SHA-256 values. scripts/rq014/preflight.py:801-864 and 1102-1209 require PASS export and DONE receipts, canonical bundle hashes, source hashes, and registry materialization before preflight can succeed.

findings.summary: The reviewed declassification code provides a strong restricted-pickle, canonical-output, semantic-denylist, timing, and receipt boundary for the first operation.
evidence: scripts/rq014/export_score_stripped_bundle.py:147-155 rejects pickle global/class construction; lines 282-326 enforce exact bundle/metadata/scene/state shapes and recursively reject rating-semantic keys; lines 413-602 use a staging directory, strict canonical outputs, full validation, and atomic publish.

scripts/rq014/preflight.py:188-242 rejects noncanonical CSV and rating-semantic columns/cell values; lines 267-650 enforce the 479-scene universe, 10 Hz/4 Hz timing, exact tstar consistency, duplicate-drop rule, candidate-set hashes, keys, counts, file hashes, and provenance receipt values.

The frozen source inventory binds producer-code audit hashes and records restricted-unpickle structural results (476 geometry scenes, 3 structural absences, exact three candidates, zero tstar mismatch on declassified scenes) without rating-value access.

findings.summary: Registry materialization is reconstructible and immutable under the reviewed 17-binding protocol.
evidence: scripts/rq014/materialize_registry.py:132-264 requires the exact binding allowlist, validates cross-registry equalities and the X02 composite, checks every source placeholder and source hash, writes once-or-identical canonical outputs, and records a complete ledger.

scripts/rq014/preflight.py:983-1099 independently reconstructs all materialized registry bytes from the immutable source registries plus binding ledger and rejects missing, aliased, drifted, or noncanonical artifacts.

findings.summary: All frozen-manifest tests passed, but they do not cover the two unresolved runtime-closure findings.
evidence: Bounded command: .venv_ipv_verifier/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_hpc_run_launcher.py tests/test_rq014_fl05_indexer.py tests/test_rq014_g0_closure_scripts.py tests/test_rq014_managed_hpc_contract.py tests/test_rq014_score_stripped_export.py tests/test_rq014_v1p3_registry_contract.py tests/test_rq014_v1p5_contract.py
Result: 81 passed in 4.62 seconds. No rating values were accessed and no HPC job was submitted.
